name: flowdeck
description: Grab a Jira task and spin up its workspace — a task pool in your sidebar that opens the right repos and pre-seeds a Claude Code agent.
author: Oz Nasi (At-Bay)

commands.title: Flow Deck: Sign in to Jira
Flow Deck: Sign out of Jira

flowdeck.jira.baseUrl.description: Base URL of your Atlassian Jira Cloud site (e.g. https://your-org.atlassian.net). Set during first-run setup.

flowdeck.jira.project.description: Jira project key to pull tasks from (e.g. ABC). Set during first-run setup.

flowdeck.reposRoot.description: Directory where your service repo checkouts live.

flowdeck.githubOrg.description: GitHub org used to clone repos that aren't checked out yet (reserved — not yet used).

flowdeck.repoBlocklist.description: Directory names under reposRoot to exclude from task-target discovery (infra, tooling, generated repos). Hidden dirs are always skipped.

flowdeck.seedAgent.description: After opening a workspace, pre-fill the Claude Code panel with the task plan (you press Enter to start).

enumDescriptions: 1 repo → open that repo; multiple → one multi-root workspace
Ask each time (when more than one repo)
flowdeck.workspaceMode.description: How to open the repos a task touches.

enumDescriptions: Ask each time you take a task
Always open a task in a new window
Always open a task in the current window (replaces what's here)
flowdeck.openIn.description: Where to open a task you take: a new window, or reuse the current one.

flowdeck.taskMode.markdownDescription: Which prompt mode to seed when taking a task: `ask` to choose each time, or the `id` of one of `#flowdeck.promptModes#`.

flowdeck.promptModes.markdownDescription: Prompt modes offered when taking a task. Each has an `id`, `label`, and `prompt` template. Placeholders: `{key}`, `{summary}`, `{url}`, `{brief}`, `{files}`. Add your own (e.g. a `debug` mode).

default.label: Plan first
default.prompt: Jira {key}: "{summary}". Read the task brief at {brief} for context and the repos involved. Propose a step-by-step PLAN for this task and wait for my approval — do not edit any code yet. Ticket: {url}{files}

default.label: Implementation
default.prompt: Jira {key}: "{summary}". Read the task brief at {brief} for context and the repos involved. Begin implementing. Confirm your approach with me only if something is ambiguous. Ticket: {url}{files}

flowdeck.explorePrompt.markdownDescription: Prompt seeded when you start an **Explore** session (no ticket). Placeholders: `{summary}` (your focus), `{brief}`, `{files}`.
flowdeck.explorePrompt.default: Exploration session — no Jira ticket yet. Focus: "{summary}". A brief listing the repos in scope is at {brief}. Help me understand how this works: map the relevant code paths, explain the flow, and flag anything surprising or worth a follow-up ticket. Don't change code unless I ask.{files}

enumDescriptions: Always create a per-task git worktree/branch
Always work in the repo directly
flowdeck.worktree.description: Whether to isolate the task in a git worktree.

flowdeck.worktreeRoot.description: Where per-task git worktrees are created (one subdir per ticket, per repo).

flowdeck.stampLabelOnWrite.description: Add the provenance label (see flowdeck.provenanceLabel) whenever this extension updates a Jira item (e.g. a status change), to mark automated changes.

flowdeck.provenanceLabel.description: Label stamped on Jira items this extension updates when flowdeck.stampLabelOnWrite is enabled.
